subject: Fwd: FTC and COUQ
body:
I sent the attached memo to you on Friday, but just in case, I am sending  it 
again.  I left a message for Steve and spoke to Jason Levin, explaining  that 
while I do not disagree with the basic theories of liability outlined in  the 
memo, what we were supposed to receive was a more detailed factual  
time-line, so that we can review the facts as a group and determine which ones  to 
present and how to present them during the telephone call with Bear's  attorney.  
Telling Bear's attorney that we will bring a claim for  fraudulent inducement 
and common law fraud for $20 MM, without going into  certain facts regarding, 
for  example, Warren's misrepresentations reckless behavior, is not  going to 
cause them to deal with us separately from everyone else.  The  presentation 
has to be specific and demonstrate unique facts to our case,  as well as 
particularly harmful facts against Bear.  In any  event, I left a message for Steve 
to call me after his deposition today and will  discuss that with steve in 
detail.  If you have anything you want to add,  please let me know.   
 
Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New  York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 

------------------------------  
IRS Circular 230 Legend: Any advice contained herein was not intended or  
written to be used, and cannot be used, for the purpose of avoiding U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax penalties. Unless otherwise specifically indicated  
above, you should assume that any statement in this email relating to any U.S.  
federal, state, or local tax matter was written in connection with the promotion 
 or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in  this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular  circumstances from an independent tax advisor.  
================================================================= 
Confidentiality  Notice: This communication and any attachments contain 
information from Darren  K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or 
that are otherwise  confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by 
the intended  recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person 
who receives  this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, 
distributing, and/or  using it. If you have received this communication in error, 
please immediately  delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at 
the above telephone  number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this 
communication is intended to  operate as an electronic signature under applicable  
law.
=================================================================



**************It's Tax Time! Get tips, forms and advice on AOL Money & 
Finance.      (http://money.aol.com/tax?NCID=aolcmp00300000002850)


From: "Jason Levin" <jlevin@samlegal.com>
Subject: FTC and COUQ
Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2008 20:24:03 GMT
To: dkiesq@aol.com
Cc: "Steven Storch" <sstorch@samlegal.com>

Darren

Attached please find three documents.  The first document is the revised
engagement letter reflecting our discussion on Tuesday.  The second
document is a redline version of the engagement letter comparing the
current version to the prior version you reviewed.  Finally, attached is
a memorandum reflecting our current thinking.  

Regards, 

Jason 

 

Jason Levin

Storch Amini & Munves PC

2 Grand Central Tower, 25th Floor

New York, New York 10017

Tel: 212.490.4100

Direct: 212.497.8218

Fax: 212.490.4208

body_html:
I sent the attached memo to you on Friday, but just in case, I am sending it again.  I left a message for Steve and spoke to Jason Levin, explaining that while I do not disagree with the basic theories of liability outlined in the memo, what we were supposed to receive was a more detailed factual time-line, so that we can review the facts as a group and determine which ones to present and how to present them during the telephone call with Bear's attorney.  Telling Bear's attorney that we will bring a claim for fraudulent inducement and common law fraud for $20 MM, without going into certain facts regarding, for example, Warren's misrepresentations reckless behavior, is not going to cause them to deal with us separately from everyone else.  The presentation has to be specific and demonstrate unique facts to our case, as well as particularly harmful facts against Bear.  In any event, I left a message for Steve to call me after his deposition today and will discuss that with steve in detail.  If you have anything you want to add, please let me know.  
 
Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com
